name: Lobios – vuelta por el río Lima y Minas das Sombras – Geres-Xures Biosphere Reserve
region_intro: Mountain biking around Lobios offers diverse routes within the Gerês-Xurês Biosphere Reserve, characterized by extensive green landscapes and varied terrain. The region features scenic water bodies like the River Lima and Salas Reservoir, providing picturesque cycling paths. Riders can explore a network of trails that traverse natural beauty spots and charming small villages. This area is ideal for those seeking mountain biking adventures amidst protected natural environments. Best mountain bike trails around Lobios The most popular mountain bike trail is Lobios – vuelta por el río Lima y Minas das Sombras – Geres-Xures Biosphere Reserve, a 25.5 miles (41.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 11 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the River Lima and the historic Minas das Sombras within the biosphere reserve. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Geira and Arrieiros Trail Loop, a difficult 27.2 miles (43.7 km) path. This route offers challenging climbs and descents through varied natural landscapes. Local mountain bikers also love the Salas Reservoir Loop from Muiños, a 19.1 miles (30.7 km) trail leading through the scenic area around the Salas river reservoir, often completed in about 2 hours 32 minutes. Mountain biking around Lobios is defined by the Gerês-Xurês Biosphere Reserve, the River Lima, and the Salas Reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult mountain bike trails. The routes in Lobios are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from 10 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Lobios's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Lobios?
A: Lobios offers a diverse network of over 20 mountain bike trails within the Gerês-Xurês Biosphere Reserve. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate paths to challenging mountain bike trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Lobios?
A: You can expect varied terrain characterized by extensive green landscapes, scenic water bodies like the River Lima and Salas Reservoir, and paths that traverse natural beauty spots and charming small villages. The region is part of the Gerês-Xurês Biosphere Reserve, ensuring a rich natural environment.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Lobios?
A: Yes, while many trails are moderate to difficult, there are 2 routes specifically rated as easy, and 8 routes rated as moderate, providing options for less experienced riders to enjoy the beautiful scenery of Lobios.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Lobios is known for its challenging routes, with 12 trails rated as difficult. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993983546/lobios-vuelta-por-el-rio-lima-y-minas-das-sombras-geres-xures-biosphere-reserve" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lobios – vuelta por el río Lima y Minas das Sombras – Geres-Xures Biosphere Reserve&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 41 km route exploring the River Lima and the historic Minas das Sombras.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while mountain biking in Lobios?
A: Lobios is rich in natural and historical attractions. You can encounter the scenic River Lima and Salas Reservoir, explore the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3112491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aquis Querquennis Roman Camp&lt;/a&gt;, or relax at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5337265" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Caldo Hot Springs&lt;/a&gt;. Other highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5957993" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cela Cavalos Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/803843" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Blue Pool&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Lobios?
A: Yes, many routes in Lobios are circular. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993771103/salas-reservoir-loop-from-muinos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salas Reservoir Loop from Muiños&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 30.7 km trail that takes you through the scenic area around the reservoir. Another challenging circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19879869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Caldo Hot Springs loop from Os Baños&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Lobios?
A: The mountain bike trails in Lobios are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from 10 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning natural beauty of the Gerês-Xurês Biosphere Reserve, and the opportunity to explore both natural and historical sites.

Q: Is there an information point for mountain bikers in Lobios?
A: Yes, for added convenience, a dedicated BTT (mountain biking) information point is available at the Caldaria de Lobios thermal spa. It offers services such as bike washing areas, changing rooms, and detailed route information.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the mountain bike trails in Lobios?
A: The region includes charming small villages such as Gustomeao, San Paio, O Puxedo, and A Vila, which you might pass through on routes like the 'Circular de Lobios'. These villages, along with the main town of Lobios, offer options for cafes, pubs, and accommodation.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Lobios?
A: Lobios offers a pleasant climate for mountain biking for much of the year. The natural hot springs, with waters ranging from 35ºC to 45ºC year-round, suggest that even cooler months can be enjoyable, especially with the reward of a thermal bath post-ride. Spring and autumn generally offer mild temperatures ideal for outdoor activities.

Q: Are there any historical sites accessible by mountain bike in Lobios?
A: Yes, several routes allow you to explore historical sites. The area includes sites like the Roman ruins of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3112491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aquis Querquennis&lt;/a&gt; in Torneiros, and religious architecture such as the San Miguel church and the Virxe da Reguenga chapel. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993983546/lobios-vuelta-por-el-rio-lima-y-minas-das-sombras-geres-xures-biosphere-reserve" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lobios – vuelta por el río Lima y Minas das Sombras&lt;/a&gt; route specifically mentions the historic Minas das Sombras.
tips: Minas das Sombras trail near Lobios, within the Gerês natural biosphere along ancient mines from Roman times. Part of the trail is very rocky and you may need to walk

Ruin of the archaeological site of the Roman villa next to the Caldo river. The path also leads to the Caldo River waterfall. The vila is part of a network of Roman vilas that reach as far as Braga in Portugal

Caldas de Lobios - Good parking to start any bike ride through the Xures Biosphere National Park. The town has a good selection of bars and restaurants and a hot thermal outdoor pool for bathing in both summer and winter.

Road that leads to the Aceredo ghost town that was covered when the Lindoso reservoir, during the dry season the town is exposed and you can cross the Lima River. If the water level is high, cross to the other side of the OU-1207 bridge on the road

waypoints.name: Roman Villa Ruins by the Río Caldo
View of the Submerged Village of Aceredo
